brand.name: National Archive
brand.value_prop: Search, browse, and request records held by the National Archive. Open public datasets, digitised historical documents, and online request forms for restricted materials.
brand.target_audience: researchers, journalists, and the general public

spec.notes:
WHAT WE'RE BUILDING (government): An official site for a government agency or municipality. Visual conventions are distinctly civic: dense forms, navy-blue + accent palette, accessibility-led, breadcrumbs on every page, plain-language copy, formal typography. The site exists to help residents complete tasks (pay a bill, request a record, find a department) — NOT to sell. Tone is neutral, formal, helpful. NO marketing language, NO 'free trial', NO testimonials. Pages have a lot of body text and structured forms. Headers include a search bar, language toggle, and accessibility settings link. BRAND: National Archive — "The public record, made accessible" Search, browse, and request records held by the National Archive. Open public datasets, digitised historical documents, and online request forms for restricted materials. VISUAL LANGUAGE (dark_native_clean): Background near-black (#0A0A0B / #111114), text near-white (#F5F5F7) at high contrast, panel surface slightly lighter (#15151A). Single accent color from the palette used sparingly: highlighted CTA, hover state, active link. Body weight ~500 — slightly heavier than typical for legibility on dark. Hairline 1px borders (#26262C) separate everything; no shadows, no rounded corners over 8px. Avoid: saturated gradients, default-Tailwind gray accents. DESIGN REFERENCES (style + topic): gov.uk, usa.gov, canada.ca, linear.app, vercel.com, stripe.com/dark PER-PAGE GUIDANCE: - index: Civic hero — name of the agency/city + brief mission. Below: top-tasks grid ('Pay a bill', 'Request a record', 'Find a department'), news/announcements section, contact strip. - contact: Multi-channel contact info: phone, email, mailing address, in-person hours, accessibility/language support contacts. - forms: List of downloadable forms or online form starts. Categorised. Each entry: form name, brief description, link to start / download. - about: Brief about-the-agency page — mission, leadership, history. Formal tone. - departments: Directory of departments. Each entry: department name, head's name + photo placeholder, contact, link to detail page. - news: Reverse-chronological list of news/announcements. Date, headline, 1-line summary, read-more link. - services: Categorised list of services. Cards or a deep table with category labels, plain-language descriptions of each service. IMAGE POLICY (HARD CONSTRAINT): - For purely decorative content (hero illustrations, abstract section dividers, decorative blobs): use NO `` tags. Use CSS gradients, inline SVG, or styled `
` blocks with `background-color` / `aspect-ratio` instead. - For content-essential photography (product photos, dish photos, hotel rooms, portrait avatars, editorial leads), use `` where X is EXCLUSIVELY one of these five filenames: photo-product-1.jpg (generic product / object — warm tones) photo-product-2.jpg (cooler product variant — for grids) photo-portrait-1.jpg (generic person — for avatars / team / bylines) photo-landscape-1.jpg (generic scene — for hero / hospitality leads) illustration-abstract.jpg (mesh-gradient — for splash / decorative hero) - Repeating a filename across multiple `` tags is fine. - ANY OTHER `src=` value will be rejected at validation time. No external URLs, no other filenames, no svg files.
